title: Define core types (Entry, Group, HostsFile)
Implement hosts-file format renderer (Entry → hosts format)
description:
Render Entry to /etc/hosts format line.

## Story Context
- **Story:** Hosts File Rendering
- **Purpose:** Enabled entries are rendered to valid /etc/hosts format
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #4 (apply mechanism works)

## Requirements
Create src/core/render.ts:
- Export renderEntry(entry: Entry): string
- Format: '<ip> <hostname> <alias1> <alias2> ...'
- Proper spacing (single space between fields)
- Optional comment at end if entry.comment exists: '# <comment>'

Example output:
```
192.168.1.100 devserver.local devserver # Development server
```

## Files
- src/core/render.ts (create)

## Dependencies
- Requires types from hosts-cli-379.1

## Verification
- Entry with no aliases: '192.168.1.1 example.com'
- Entry with aliases: '192.168.1.1 example.com ex ex.local'
- Entry with comment: '192.168.1.1 example.com # My server'
- IPv6 works: '::1 localhost'

title: Implement managed block wrapper (add BEGIN/END markers)
description:
Wrap rendered entries in managed block markers.

## Story Context
- **Story:** Hosts File Rendering
- **Purpose:** Enabled entries are rendered to valid /etc/hosts format
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #4 (apply mechanism works)

## Requirements
Add to src/core/render.ts:
- Export wrapManagedBlock(content: string): string
- Add '# BEGIN HOSTIE' header
- Add '# END HOSTIE' footer
- Include blank line before and after content for readability

Example output:
```
# BEGIN HOSTIE

192.168.1.100 devserver.local devserver
192.168.1.101 staging.local staging

# END HOSTIE
```

## Files
- src/core/render.ts (modify)

## Dependencies
- Requires types from hosts-cli-379.1

## Verification
- Markers are on separate lines
- Content is between markers
- Blank lines improve readability

title: Implement enabled-only filtering
description:
Filter entries to only include enabled ones.

## Story Context
- **Story:** Hosts File Rendering
- **Purpose:** Enabled entries are rendered to valid /etc/hosts format
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #4 (apply mechanism works)

## Requirements
Add to src/core/render.ts:
- Export renderHostsFile(hostsFile: HostsFile): string
- Recursively traverse groups and collect all enabled entries
- Render each enabled entry using renderEntry()
- Wrap in managed block using wrapManagedBlock()
- Flatten nested groups (groups are organizational only, not functional per D4)

- src/core/render.ts (modify)

- Requires types from hosts-cli-379.1

## Verification
- Only enabled entries appear in output
- Disabled entries are excluded
- Nested groups are flattened correctly
- Output is wrapped in managed block

title: Implement atomic write (temp file + rename)

title: Implement idempotency check (only write if changed)
description:
Skip write if content unchanged.

## Story Context
- **Story:** Apply Mechanism
- **Purpose:** Users can apply their ~/.hosts changes to /etc/hosts safely
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #4 (apply mechanism works)

## Requirements
Add to src/core/apply.ts:
- Export applyHostsFile(hostsFile: HostsFile): Promise<{ changed: boolean, message: string }>
- Read current /etc/hosts
- Render new managed block from hostsFile
- Compare new content with current content
- Only write if different
- Return status message

## Files
- src/core/apply.ts (create)

## Verification
- Skips write if content unchanged
- Writes if content changed
- Returns accurate status message
- Handles first-time application

title: Implement sudo re-exec (detect EACCES, re-run with sudo)
description:
Re-execute with sudo on permission error.

## Story Context
- **Story:** Apply Mechanism
- **Purpose:** Users can apply their ~/.hosts changes to /etc/hosts safely
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #4 (apply mechanism works)

## Requirements
Add to src/core/apply.ts:
- Detect EACCES error when writing /etc/hosts
- Re-exec current process with sudo
- Pass through all original arguments
- Preserve stdin/stdout/stderr
- Exit with sudo process exit code

Use Bun.spawn() for re-exec:
```typescript
const result = Bun.spawn(['sudo', Bun.argv[0], ...Bun.argv.slice(1)], {
  stdio: ['inherit', 'inherit', 'inherit']
});
await result.exited;
process.exit(result.exitCode);
```

## Files
- src/core/apply.ts (modify)

## Verification
- Detects EACCES correctly
- Re-execs with sudo
- Preserves arguments
- User is prompted for password once
- Works in interactive and non-interactive contexts

title: Implement hostname validation (RFC 952/1123)
description:
Implement hostname validation according to RFC 952/1123.

## Story Context
- **Story:** Data Foundation
- **Purpose:** Users' host entries are represented as typed data structures with validation
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #5 (validation enforced)

## Requirements
Create src/domain/validators.ts with validateHostname function:
- Length: 1-255 characters (63 per label)
- Characters: a-z, A-Z, 0-9, hyphen, period
- First character: letter or digit (RFC 1123)
- Last character: not hyphen or period
- No consecutive periods
- Case insensitive

## Files
- src/domain/validators.ts (create)

## Verification
- Valid hostnames pass: localhost, example.com, dev-server.local, 1server.com
- Invalid hostnames fail: -invalid, invalid-, .invalid, invalid..com, empty string

title: Implement ~/.hosts file I/O (read, write, create if missing)
description:
Implement file I/O for ~/.hosts.

## Story Context
- **Story:** File Persistence
- **Purpose:** Users' entries are saved to and loaded from ~/.hosts in YAML format
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #3 (data persistence)

## Requirements
Create src/core/file-io.ts:
- Export readHostsFile(): Promise<HostsFile> - reads ~/.hosts, creates default if missing
- Export writeHostsFile(data: HostsFile): Promise<void> - writes to ~/.hosts
- Use Bun.file() API for file operations
- Expand ~ to home directory

Default HostsFile when creating new file:
```yaml
version: 1
groups: []
```

## Files
- src/core/file-io.ts (create)

## Dependencies
- Requires types from hosts-cli-379.1

## Verification
- readHostsFile creates ~/.hosts if missing
- readHostsFile returns parsed HostsFile
- writeHostsFile persists changes
- File permissions are 644

title: Implement schema version handling (v1 only for now)
description:
Implement schema version validation.

## Story Context
- **Story:** File Persistence
- **Purpose:** Users' entries are saved to and loaded from ~/.hosts in YAML format
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #3 (data persistence)

## Requirements
Add to src/core/yaml.ts:
- Validate version field exists and equals 1
- Throw error with upgrade instructions if version > 1
- Throw error if version < 1 or missing

Error messages:
- Missing version: 'Invalid ~/.hosts: missing version field'
- Unsupported version: 'Unsupported ~/.hosts version X. This tool supports version 1. Please upgrade hostie.'

## Files
- src/core/yaml.ts (modify)

## Dependencies

- Version 2+ files throw upgrade error
- Missing version throws error
